terms.title: Terms of Use
terms.wip_notice: This is a preliminary text. The final Terms of Use content will be elaborated and reviewed by a legal team at a later date.
terms.section1_content: [Content to be defined] Using Mitra Privacy Tool implies full acceptance of these Terms of Use. If you do not agree with any provision herein, we recommend you do not use the platform.
terms.section2_content: [Content to be defined] Mitra Privacy Tool is a software privacy evaluation platform aimed at researchers, evaluators, and development teams. The tool offers structured inspections based on the Mitra Method.
terms.section3_content: [Content to be defined] The user is responsible for the accuracy of the registration information provided, for safeguarding their access credentials, and for appropriate use of the platform in accordance with these terms.
terms.section4_content: [Content to be defined] All content and features made available by the platform are protected by applicable intellectual property laws. Unauthorized reproduction or distribution is prohibited.
terms.section5_content: [Content to be defined] The platform is provided 'as is', without guarantees of uninterrupted availability. The maintainers are not liable for damages resulting from the use or inability to use the service.
terms.section6_content: [Content to be defined] These terms may be updated periodically. Users will be notified of relevant changes. Continued use of the platform after notification implies acceptance of the new conditions.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.wip_notice: This is a preliminary text. The final Privacy Policy content will be elaborated and reviewed by a legal team specialized in data protection law.
privacy.section1_content: [Content to be defined] Information about the party responsible for processing personal data collected by the Mitra Privacy Tool platform will be detailed in this section.
privacy.section2_content: [Content to be defined] We collect data necessary for the platform's operation, including name, professional email, and usage data. We do not collect sensitive data beyond what is strictly necessary.
privacy.section3_content: [Content to be defined] Collected data is used for: platform authentication and access, service communications, continuous improvement of features, and compliance with legal obligations.
privacy.section4_content: [Content to be defined] Your data is not sold or shared with third parties for commercial purposes. It may be shared only when necessary for service operation or by legal obligation.
privacy.section5_content: [Content to be defined] In accordance with applicable data protection laws, you have rights such as: access to your data, correction, deletion, portability, and withdrawal of consent. Contact us to exercise them.
privacy.section6_content: [Content to be defined] The platform uses essential cookies for authentication and operation. Details about the types of cookies used and how to manage them will be described in this section.
privacy.section7_content: [Content to be defined] For questions, requests, or exercising privacy-related rights, please contact us through the channels that will be provided in this section.

publication.create_message: This consolidated round will be published to the public directory. Choose the visibility level.

footer.description: An initiative to enhance adherence to technical best practices for personal data and empower transparency.
welcome.title: Mitra Tool - Privacy Assessment Tool
welcome.hero_description: Based on LGPD and the TR-Model, this tool empowers users to make more informed decisions and helps developers adhere to software privacy compliance.
welcome.challenge_text: This project addresses the lack of practical and accessible tools for evaluating the transparency of personal data handling practices. The goal is to propose an objective tool for inspecting Personal Data Transparency in software applications.
welcome.feature1_text: Assessment based on 46 thorough criteria, logically organized into 5 transparency dimensions.
welcome.feature2_text: Fully aligned with Brazilian data protection legislation, ensuring your software operates within fiscal requirements.
welcome.feature3_text: Get visual scores, medal classifications, and the ability to export JSON reports for each evaluated dimension.
welcome.methodology_intro: Our inspection evaluates in detail {count} transparency dimensions to provide a complete overview of your product's compliance:
welcome.methodology_explanation: Each dimension is measured in two categories (Information Existence and Quality and Presentation Format). The result indicates the compliance level through a gamified medal-based system:
welcome.features_subtitle: An interface designed to reduce friction and maximize technical clarity.
welcome.feat_forms_text: Agile inspection filling in sections with scales of "Sufficient", "Insufficient", "Non-existent", simplifying decision-making.
welcome.feat_charts_text: Immediate processing of the final score per section based on responses, accompanied by intuitive visual panels.
welcome.feat_export_text: Export validated responses to JSON format. Modern interface designed for optimal visual adaptation (light/dark mode according to your preference*).
welcome.mitra_text1: The Mitra Method is the heart of this tool. It offers a structured checklist for evaluating transparency in the handling of personal data, focusing on both information quality and display format.
welcome.mitra_text2: Based on the TR-Model and academically validated, the method allows classifying software by compliance and privacy usability levels.
welcome.mitra_learn_more: Learn About the Method in Detail
welcome.directory_text: We believe transparency should reach the end user. That's why we offer a Public Directory where developers can choose to publish their compliance results.
welcome.directory_open_query_text: Any user can check the transparency medal of their favorite tool.
welcome.directory_history_text: Track the privacy evolution of software across different inspection rounds.
welcome.developed_with: Developed with Laravel v{laravel} & PHP v{php}
welcome.footer_description: An initiative to increase adherence to best practices in personal data handling and empower transparency.
mitra.hero_description: A systematic approach to inspect and classify transparency in personal data handling. The Mitra Method bridges the gap between legal requirements (such as LGPD) and their practical implementation in computing systems.
mitra.what_is_text: It is an inspection method based on the TR-Model and the Six Usable Privacy Heuristics. It uses a structured checklist (TR-Checklist) to identify critical transparency gaps.
mitra.for_whom_text: Developers, designers, and auditors seeking to create "transparent by design" systems, ensuring end users understand how their data is handled.
mitra.dim1_text: Clear identification of who collects (Controller), who processes (Operator), and which third parties access the data.
mitra.dim2_text: Clear, specific, and legitimate explanation of the purposes for collecting each type of data.
mitra.dim3_text: Details about which categories of data are collected (sensitive, browsing, cookies, etc.).
mitra.dim4_text: Transparency about data flow to other organizations or international transfers.
mitra.dim5_text: Means for the user to seek information, question usage, or withdraw consent easily.
mitra.medals_description: The inspection result consolidates a score that classifies the application into four transparency levels:
manual.hero_desc: Complete guide to all features of Mitra Tool — the personal data transparency inspection tool based on LGPD and the Mitra Method.
manual.hero_stats: 46 criteria · 5 dimensions · 2 evaluation sections · Medal system
manual.workflow_desc: From registration to publishing — the complete path to evaluate your software's transparency.
manual.medals_desc: The result consolidates a score that classifies the application into four transparency levels:
manual.section_i_desc: Evaluates whether transparency information exists and has sufficient quality for user understanding.
manual.section_ii_desc: Evaluates whether information is presented in an accessible, objective, and understandable way.
manual.detailed_desc: Click each section to expand details.
manual.tip_desc: For more reliable results, it is recommended that multiple evaluators fill out the questionnaire independently within the same round. The consolidated result (average of responses) provides a more impartial view of the evaluated software's transparency level.
manual.role_owner_desc: Full control: create, edit, delete, invite members, create rounds, publish results.
manual.role_evaluator_desc: Create inspections, fill questionnaires, view results.
manual.role_observer_desc: View-only access to the project and its results.
manual.answer_sufficient_desc: The information is present and adequate.
manual.answer_insufficient_desc: The information exists but is incomplete or inadequate.
manual.answer_na_desc: The question does not apply to the context.
manual.dim_1_desc: Identification of involved actors: controller, operator, and regulatory agencies.
manual.dim_2_desc: Objectives, legal basis, processing period, and legal representative.
manual.dim_3_desc: What data is collected, its origin, composition, and permissions.
manual.dim_4_desc: Transfer and sharing policies with third parties.
manual.dim_5_desc: Means for individuals to exercise their rights over data.
manual.wf_1_desc: Register the software to be evaluated with name, description, and URL.
manual.wf_2_desc: Send email invitations to evaluators and observers.
manual.wf_3_desc: Organize inspections into thematic evaluation rounds.
manual.wf_4_desc: Each member creates their individual inspection in the round.
manual.wf_5_desc: Answer 46 questions on a scale (Sufficient / Insufficient / Nonexistent).
manual.wf_6_desc: Close inspections and rounds to generate result snapshots.
manual.wf_7_desc: Individual, team, and round-consolidated scores with medals.
manual.wf_8_desc: Publish to the Public Directory and generate verifiable badges for embedding.
manual.sec_cadastro_text1: On the home page, click "Sign Up". Fill in Name, Email, and Password (minimum 8 characters). Confirm the password and submit the form.
manual.sec_cadastro_text2: On the home page, click "Log In". Enter your registered email and password. The system supports "Remember Me" and password recovery by email.
manual.sec_cadastro_text3: Accessible from "Profile" in the top menu. Allows changing Name, Email, and Password. Includes the option to permanently delete the account.
manual.sec_projetos_text1: In the Workspace, click "New Project". Fill in: Name (required — name of the software being evaluated), Description, Software URL, Icon, and Highlight Color. You will be automatically added as the owner.
manual.sec_projetos_text2: Displays general information, members and their roles, pending invitations, evaluation rounds, and inspections associated with the project.
manual.sec_projetos_text3: The owner can edit data or delete the project at any time (deletion is reversible via soft delete).
manual.sec_projetos_text4: In Settings, the owner can require mandatory evidence for positive answers, set the visibility of individual evaluations, and choose the consensus model (Owner Decides, Convergence, or Majority Vote).
manual.sec_convites_text1: On the project screen, enter the invitee's email and select the role (Evaluator or Observer). The system sends an email with an acceptance link valid for 7 days.
manual.sec_convites_text2: If the invitee already has an account, they are automatically added to the project. If not, the link directs them to create an account linked to the invitation email.
manual.sec_convites_text3: The owner can change roles between Evaluator and Observer. The Owner role cannot be changed. Invitations can be resent (renews the deadline) or canceled.
manual.sec_rodadas_text1: On the project screen, click "New Evaluation Round". Enter a descriptive name (e.g., "Q1 2026 Assessment"). The round is created with Draft status.
manual.sec_rodadas_text2: Draft → Closed. In draft state, inspections can be created and filled out. When closed, the consolidated snapshot is generated automatically.
manual.sec_rodadas_text3: Click "Review and Close" to see a result preview. Optionally add a textual diagnosis. Check if you want to publish immediately to the Public Directory and choose the visibility (Score Only or Full Report).
manual.sec_rodadas_text4: When closing a round with divergent scores, the configured Consensus Model is applied. The Divergence Panel classifies discrepancies into Low, Medium, and High levels based on statistical variance. Under the Evaluator Convergence model, members can debate via an integrated chat (Conflict Thread) for each question in conflict and update their scores until consensus is achieved.
manual.sec_inspecoes_text1: An inspection represents an individual member's assessment of the software. Each inspection is linked to an Evaluation Round and a questionnaire version.
manual.sec_inspecoes_text2: On the project or round screen, click "New Inspection" and select the round. The inspection is created with Draft status.
manual.sec_inspecoes_text3: Draft → Active (click "Activate") → Closed (click "Close"). During the Active phase, fill out the questionnaire. When closed, individual and consolidated snapshots are generated. Only the responsible person (creator) can change the status. Closed inspections cannot be reopened.
manual.sec_questionario_text1: The questionnaire contains 46 questions organized in 2 sections (Information Existence and Quality with 26 questions; Presentation Format with 20 questions), each evaluating the 5 transparency dimensions.
manual.sec_questionario_text2: Access the inspection with Active status. The questionnaire is presented in cards by section and category. For each question, select Sufficient (100 pts), Insufficient (50 pts), or Nonexistent (0 pts). Optionally, add observations justifying your choice.
manual.sec_resultados_text1: Shows your personal assessment result: score by section, by dimension, and final medal. Access via project screen → inspection → "View My Results".
manual.sec_resultados_text2: Consolidated average of all evaluators' responses in the inspection. Available only when the inspection is closed.
manual.sec_resultados_text3: Displays the consolidated average of all evaluator responses within a single inspection or round.
manual.sec_resultados_text4:
1. Category: round((sum(points) / (total * 100)) * 100)
2. Section: Average of categories.
3. Global Score: Simple average of all sections, rounded, determining the final medal.
manual.sec_diretorio_text1: After closing the round, click "Publish to Public Directory". Choose: Score Only (displays scores and medals) or Full Report (complete breakdown by question). Publishing generates a unique slug (friendly URL).
manual.sec_diretorio_text2: Access /tools without login. Filter by medal, year, or questionnaire version. Sort by score or date. Click to view the public report.
manual.sec_diretorio_text3: The system automatically sanitizes public data, removing user_id, observations, and comments to protect evaluators' privacy.
manual.sec_diretorio_text4: The public report showcases key transparency metrics of the assessment process, including the Audit Type (Self-Assessment or External Audit), the audited software version, the total number of participating evaluators, and the Consensus Model used to consolidate the scores.
manual.sec_selos_text1: On the published round screen, click "Generate Badge". The system creates a badge with a unique public token.
manual.sec_selos_text2: Default (complete card with name, medal, score, and date), Compact (compact version), and Minimal (medal and score in line only).
manual.sec_selos_text3: Paste the snippet on any website. The script loads updated data via API (5-min cache) and renders automatically.
manual.sec_selos_text4: The owner can revoke the badge at any time, making it inactive.
manual.sec_exportacao_text1: On the project screen, click "Export Project". Generates a JSON file with all data: project, members, rounds, inspections, responses, and questions.
manual.sec_exportacao_text2: In "Profile", click "Export All Data". Generates a ZIP file with a JSON for each project you participate in.
settings.title: Project Settings
settings.description: Configure the tool behavior for this project.
settings.evidence_description: Require a text explanation (observation) for answers marked as 'Sufficient (100 pts)'.
settings.consensus_description: Defines how divergent scores between evaluators are resolved to consolidate the round score.
settings.consensus_owner_decides_desc: The owner decides the final score if there are disagreements among evaluators.
settings.consensus_convergence_desc: Evaluators discuss in threads and adjust their own scores until convergence is achieved.
settings.consensus_majority_vote_desc: The score is calculated based on the majority vote of the evaluators.
settings.audit_description: Set whether this assessment is considered an internal self-assessment or external audit.
settings.evaluations_visibility_descript: By default, each evaluator only sees their own evaluation to avoid influencing others. Enable this option to allow all members to see evaluations already completed.
settings.show_evaluations_to_all: Show all evaluators' assessments to project members
round.observer_no_action: As an observer, you can follow inspections but cannot create or modify evaluations.
review.evaluations_hidden: Individual evaluations are not visible to preserve evaluator independence.
divergence.no_questions: No divergence found with the selected filters.
review.resolution_owner_instructions: As the project owner, you have the power to decide the final score for this question.
review.resolution_owner_nonowner_info: The project owner will define the final consolidated response.
review.resolution_pending: Waiting for the Owner's final decision.
review.majority_vote_desc: The final score is automatically calculated by the majority of votes. Ties are broken in favor of the most conservative score (lowest).
review.tie_broken_conservative: Tie broken in favor of the most conservative score.
review.select_question_prompt: Select a question from the list to open the Resolution Workspace.
manual.sec_visao_text1: The Mitra Privacy Tool enables teams to evaluate a software's transparency regarding personal data processing. The workflow covers everything from project creation to public reporting and badge generation.
manual.sec_visao_text2: The entire user interface, system messages, and emails operate dynamically in three languages: Portuguese, English, and Spanish.
manual.sec_workspace_text1: Upon logging in, you will find a list of all your active projects, pending invitations waiting for your approval, and a shortcut to create new projects.
manual.sec_comparacao_text1: Compare two closed individual evaluations to spot discrepancies and agreements between auditors.
manual.sec_comparacao_text2: Compare final consolidated reports of two closed rounds, tracking the software's transparency evolution over time.
manual.sec_admin_text1: Administrators can manage users, oversee all projects, edit questionnaire versions, manage invitations, and audit system logs.
manual.sec_mitra_text1: The Mitra Privacy Tool is based on the TR-Model, structured around two major sections: Information Existence and Quality, and Presentation Format.
manual.sec_mitra_text2: The framework splits privacy into: People/Actors, Purpose of Use, Personal Data, Sharing, and Agency (individual rights).
manual.sec_fluxo_text1: The official flowchart details every step, from creating an inspection to closing with consensus, publication, and badge deployment.
review.convergence_instructions: Evaluators should discuss below to align interpretations. The round will be consolidated by average scores once the owner closes.
review.final_diagnosis_desc: Write a general conclusion on the product privacy level based on consensus reached.
review.close_warning: Upon closing, scores will be consolidated according to resolutions and final score will be generated.
review.delete_comment_confirm: Do you really want to delete this comment?
review.no_comments: No comments submitted yet. Share your observations to seek consensus.
